parameters.content:
## AutoLead - Leadgen - TBA

**Purpose:**
- The AutoLead workflow automates the population of user fields in the Leadgen section of Madmin by retrieving data from Intercom and ActiveCampaign. It checks whether a publisher name exists on these platforms and, if found, automatically fills in the corresponding fields in Leadgen.

## Important URL
**Google Sheets (production)**:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1kVe9Iyy4PL9LxCf8PzRzjdQ8XoEsU7P7qAdeVFCcLLU/edit?gid=0#gid=0

**Production URL**
https://neightn.indie-demo.com/form/656cf243-8999-45ef-b178-ae322165231b

## Documentation
- This workflow will only trigger when the user types Run in the input field of the form

- In Google Sheets, it will only get rows with Active status

- Using the publisher name, it will check Intercom to see if the publisher already exists. If yes, it will save the contact.

- Check if publisher name exist on ActiveCampaign, if yes, get the contact, save it

- If publisher name does not exist on both platform, save it in No Result

parameters.query:
SELECT
  id,
  product_source_id,
  publisher_name,
  contact_product_name,
  'match' as result_type
FROM system_leads
WHERE publisher_name = $1
  AND (
        contact_product_name = $2
        OR $2 IS NULL
      )

UNION ALL

SELECT
  NULL as id,
  NULL as product_source_id,
  $1 as publisher_name,
  $2 as contact_product_name,
  'no_match' as result_type
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM system_leads
  WHERE publisher_name = $1
    AND (
          contact_product_name = $2
          OR $2 IS NULL
        )
)

LIMIT 1

nodes.name: Madmin
nodes.notes: This query checks if the publisher name or product name already exists in the system_leads table. If a match is found, it returns the existing record and marks it as "match". If no match is found, it returns a single row with empty values and marks it as "no_match", and it only returns one result.

nodes.name: Intercom: Search Contacts.
nodes.notes: Check if the publisher_name input is match with the custom_attributes.Publishers field from Intercom

nodes.name: Data Null
nodes.notes:
This code checks the Intercom API results and finds contacts that match the publisher name from your database. 

Condition:
1. Filters contacts to make sure their external_id(user_id) starts with "p_" 
2. publisher_name input must be equal to publisher / developer field on Intercom

If matching contacts are found, it returns their details and shows that Intercom has a match. 

If no contacts are found, it returns an empty list and shows that no Intercom match exists, along with the publisher name that was searched.

nodes.name: Loop Intercom
nodes.notes: Do not change the name of this node

nodes.name: Loop ActiveCampaign
nodes.notes: Do not change the name of this node

nodes.name: Match
nodes.notes:
This code checks if the publisher_name input is match with the ActiveCampaign org_name


If a match is found, it returns the organization details and shows "found".

If no match is found, it returns "not found" and shows the original publisher name that was searched.

nodes.name: Tracker: Not Found v2
nodes.notes: - If publisher_name is not found on ActiveCampaign, get the publisher_name and add a tracker "not found"

nodes.name: Reference
nodes.notes:
This code checks if the publisher name from the current input exists in the “Input Modify” node data. 

If a match is found, it returns the product name, publisher name, and URLs from that row. 

If no match is found, it returns empty values but keeps the original publisher name and shows a message that the publisher was not found.

nodes.name: AC: Get Details of Single Contact

parameters.content:
## Trigger Node
- This workflow will trigger when you type "Run" in the form.

## Logic Node
- Check if the user type "Run" if yes get the data in sheet, if no, stop the workflow.

## Action Node
- Get the data from Input sheet, AutoLead Document.
- Check if the status is active

## Data Transform Node
- Change the publisher / developer name column into publisher_name

## Action Node
- Check the publisher_name and product_name in Madmin

## Logic Node
- Loop through Madmin output

## Action Node
- Check the publisher_name on Intercom

## Data Transform Node
- Check the json output and implement a condition

## Logic Node
- Check if the intercom_contacts array field is null or not null

## Data Transform Node
- If intercom_contacts is null, get the publisher_name and add a tracker "not found"

## Output Node - Save
- Update the autolead document, input sheet, status column into 'complete'

- complete means it found a contact for that publisher / developer name

## Output Node - Save
- Append the data we get from Intercom into autolead document, intercom output sheet

## Logic Node
- Loop through tracker data.

## Action Node
- Check the publisher_name on ActiveCampaign

## Data Transform Node
- Check the json output and implement a matching condition

## Logic Node
- Check the message field
## Data Transform Node

## Output Node - Save
- Update the autolead document, input sheet, status column into 'complete - not found'

- complete - not found means it doesn't found a publisher_name both intercom and activecampaign

## Data Transform Node
- Create a reference from the 'Input Modify' node

## Output Node - Save
- Append the data that not exist in autolead document, no result output sheet.

## Action Node
- Get the organization id to get the contact

## Data Transform Node
- Flatten AccountContacts field

## Action Node
- Get single details of contact

## Output Node - Save
- Update the autolead document, input sheet, status column into 'complete'

- complete means it found a publisher_name on activecampaign

## Output Node - Save
- Append the data we get from ActiveCampaign into autolead document, activecampaign output sheet

## Data Transform Node
- If intercom_contacts is not null, get the publisher_name and add a tracker "found"

## Data Transform Node
- Get all the data from previous node
# Intercom Output

## Data Transform Node
- Get only the data who has a 'not found' in their tracker field
# ActiveCampaign Output

## Data Transform Node
- Get only the data who has a 'not found' in their tracker field
# No Result Output

nodes.name: Get data from v1 and v2

parameters.content:
## Data Transform Node
- Get the data from Filter Tracker v1 and v2
- Return the data only if the data not found in V1 is still not found in V2.

## Logic Node
- Loop through data

parameters.completionMessage: Automation ran successfully. Please check the results.

nodes.name: Get Matching Id and Account
nodes.notes: match the account field and id field, if match return the data, if no match meaning the accountContacts is empty(there's an org but no contact), return it as no found

parameters.content: ## Logic Node

nodes.name: Matching Account Name
nodes.notes:
check the account.name
match with publisher / developer name in Input node
if there's a match change the account.name into publisher / developer  name casing

this will solve the issue about case sensitive in user input and active campaign response

Get data from v1 and v2
